curator: Claude (launch demo set — see CURATION.md; reals sourced + dated, verify before public seeding)

quotes.text: I don't know if those are B's. They might be Cardi D's. I'm pretty sure those aren't B's. She's got the wrong initials.
quotes.speaker: Charles Barkley
quotes.context: on the TNT desk during Cardi B's halftime show, NBA Finals Game 3

source.title: Larry Brown Sports — Charles Barkley's wild Cardi B quote

quotes.text: I measured the rim before the game. It is exactly regulation height. I was a little disappointed — I had hoped it would be slightly higher, for the challenge.
quotes.speaker: Victor Wembanyama
quotes.context: reportedly before Game 3 of the NBA Finals
quotes.fake_note: Fake. Wemby really does say poetic, eccentric things, which is exactly why this one is hard — the voice is right, the fact is invented. No, he did not measure the rim.

quotes.text: What would take Hubble 2,000 years to process, Roman can do in a year.
quotes.speaker: Jared Isaacman
quotes.context: NASA Administrator, on the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope

source.title: Scientific American — NASA's Roman Space Telescope will launch in September

quotes.text: Statistically, there should be a planet out there made almost entirely of diamond. We've quietly decided not to go looking for it — because the day we find it, someone is going to want to go get it.
quotes.speaker: a NASA astrophysicist
quotes.context: supposedly at a Roman telescope media briefing
quotes.fake_note: Fake. 'Diamond planets' are a real hypothesis, which is what sells the lie — but no NASA scientist said this, and 'we decided not to look' is the giveaway.

quotes.text: I appreciate that many of us grew up and enjoyed science fiction, but it's not helpful.
quotes.speaker: Jensen Huang
quotes.context: Nvidia CEO, on quantifying the existential risk of AI

source.title: TechRadar — Quote of the day: Jensen Huang on AI risk

quotes.text: The new Siri can now tell when you're about to say something you'll regret, and it waits half a second before sending it. Internally, we call it the grace period.
quotes.speaker: Tim Cook
quotes.context: supposedly at his final WWDC keynote as CEO
quotes.fake_note: Fake. Cook's last WWDC keynote and a big Siri overhaul were both real this month — so the setting is true, which makes the invented feature land. There is no 'grace period.'
